title: Aleph-class starfighter
content:
{{Top|leg|title=''Aleph''-class starfighter}} {{Youmay|the Aleph-class starfighter|[[Aleph (disambiguation)|other uses of the term Aleph]]}} {{StarshipClass |image= |name=''Aleph''-class starfighter |designer= |manufacturer=[[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' |line= |model=''Aleph''-class [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] |type=Starfighter |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=[[Interceptor/Legends|Interceptor]]-grade |maneuverability= |engine=Twin thruster pods |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput=High |power=High-powered generators |shield gen=Equipped |hull=Extra-thick hull plating |sensor= |target=Equipped |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*[[Quad laser cannon/Legends|Quad laser cannon]] turrets (2) *[[Proton torpedo/Legends|Proton torpedo]] launcher (1) *[[Concussion missile/Legends|Concussion missile]] launcher (1) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=*[[Pilot/Legends|Pilot]] (1) *[[Gunner/Legends|Gunner]] (1) *[[Astromech droid/Legends|Astromech droid]] (1) |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=*Starfighter *Picket''[[Fate of the Jedi: Backlash]]'' |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] **[[Galactic Alliance Defense Force|Galactic Alliance Navy]] }} {{Quote|I've played around with Aleph simulators. They've got plenty of speed, but they maneuver like big plugs of duracrete.|Syal Antilles|audio=SyalAleph.flac|}} The '''''Aleph''-class starfighter''', originally nicknamed the '''Pondskipper''', but more commonly known as the '''Aleph''' or '''Twee''', was a heavy two-seater [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] designed by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] for use by the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet|Galactic Alliance Navy]]. The fighter was utilized by the Alliance during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] and beyond. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|I hit mine with a good quad-linked punch and it didn't even shudder.''"
"''Yeah, they're supposed to be like shooting solid metal ball bearings.|Han Solo and Wedge Antilles|audio=HanWedgeAleph.flac|}} As the nickname "Twee" suggested, an Aleph resembled the head of a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] with two long, tapering drive units thrust back behind a ball-shaped cockpit pod, similar to that of a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] but somewhat larger. To add to the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter's]] ungainly appearance, the cockpit was flanked by a [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser]] turret on either side, likened by some personnel to a pair of earmuffs. These turrets were usually problematic to operate, and would often either fail to immediately respond or jam altogether. The [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] sat side-by-side in a shared [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] with two forward-facing circular [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]]. The gunner sat on the right and the pilot on the left, with an [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] set centrally in a socket behind their respective seats. The Aleph's large hull was very tough—an armored chassis housed mighty power generators to feed its strong [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. Its main weapons were the aforementioned quad [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], which boasted very wide fields of fire and an option to un-link the cannons, creating an unpredictable spray pattern; this was a holdover from the Aleph's original purpose. In addition, the fighter featured forward-firing [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] tubes. The fighter also had an impressive top speed, comparable to the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] [[Eta-5 interceptor]], and was quick in atmospheres—although its acceleration paled in comparison to that of an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]—but its one major deficiency was a serious lack of maneuverability. To compensate for this, rows of thruster ports were placed along the dorsal and ventral surfaces and both sides of the hull, allowing the fighter to make lateral sidesteps or vertical jumps and drops in flight. However, many pilots regarded this system as no substitute for the inherent grace and maneuverability of a smaller [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighter]]. ''Aleph''-class starfighters also relied on a transport vessel such as a [[Mon Calamari heavy carrier]] to ferry them into and out of battle. ==History== ===Inception and production=== {{Quote|Sienar ''Aleph''-class starfighters. Originally nicknamed Pondskippers because they were to be countermeasures to coralskippers. The current nickname is Twees. They're out of prototype and in limited production.|Wedge Antilles elaborating on the Aleph's design|}} In the last [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' plans were proposed by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] to create a specialized fightercraft for battling the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yorik-et]], or "coralskipper", on a one-to-one basis, earning the design the nickname "Pondskipper". However, the design would not be seen in action before the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] won the war, and it would be more than a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] before the Aleph would be eventually produced in limited numbers for the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet|Galactic Alliance Navy]], around [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. As of this time, six Alephs were combined in an [[Gray Squadron (Aleph-class)|experimental]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] and based on the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] heavy [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] ''[[Blue Diver]]'' in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]]. [[Syal Antilles]] flew as the pilot in an ''Aleph''-class starfighter, although she would have preferred to fly a much faster Eta-5 [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]]. [[Zueb Zan]] was her gunner, and would complain with regularity about the turrets, which were often balky, and the general maneuverability of the vehicle, complaints with which Antilles concurred. Even so, Antilles reflected that her [[Antilles family/Legends|family]] name deserved a little prestige, and so she decided to test the Aleph to its limits. Stationed at [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]] as the conflict with [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] escalated, [[Gray Four|their vessel]] participated in the [[Battle of Tralus (Second Galactic Civil War)|battle]] that opened the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]; during the fighting and a close encounter with [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] sympathizers [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and Syal's own father [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the craft was damaged and narrowly evaded destruction, and Antilles managed to return to the ''Blue Diver'' despite the wounds dealt to the fighter. The two also served throughout the war and it was [[Battle of the rendezvous point|in battle]] with the ''Aleph''-class design that Antilles would be elevated to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] after she and Zan defended Galactic Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] against a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[StealthX]] squadron led by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] after a failed Corellian assassination attempt from [[Centerpoint Station]] destroyed the ''Blue Diver'' and left the Galactic Alliance fleet in tatters.''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'' ===Later use=== {{Quote|If they want to arrest us, they would have sent something that could catch us.|Han Solo as two ''Aleph''-class starfighters form up on the ''Millennium Falcon'' over Coruscant|audio=HanAleph.flac|}} Alephs were also used to patrol [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] orbit as of [[43 ABY]];''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' as Han Solo and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] journeyed there in an attempt to heal a rift between the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] and the Galactic Alliance, two of these ships directed the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. At least one was assigned to the [[Galactic Alliance Starfighter Command|Starfighter Command]] contingent on [[Rimsaw Station]], orbiting the [[Wild Space]] planet of [[Kuratooine]], in [[44 ABY]]. Bearing the [[Color/Legends|gray]] markings of Starfighter Command, the Aleph was deployed to the planet to support [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Kadana Sorrel]] in her investigation of rogue [[General/Legends|General]] [[Stavin Thaal]] during [[Wraith Squadron]]'s [[Mission to Kuratooine|campaign]] to root him out.''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The ''Aleph''-class starfighter was introduced in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' by [[Aaron Allston]], the first novel in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series. Since then, the fighter has also made appearances in three more of Allston's novels: ''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'' and the [[Star Wars: Fate of the Jedi|''Fate of the Jedi'']] novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Backlash]]'', as well as ''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]''. In ''[[The Written Word]]'' Hyperspace article, Hebrew letters are associated with the Sith language, and it is stated that the Aleph fighter looks similar to the [[Myke (language)|Myke]] letter Aleph.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} The name "Twee" partially came from Aaron Allston's favorite phony "[[Darth/Legends|Darth]]" name for [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], "Darth Twee."{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/novel/f20070226/index.html|text=Aaron Allston: Examining ''Exile''}} ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' {{1st|[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]}} *[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Betrayal'' abridged audiobook]] {{1st|''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'' *[[Legacy of the Force: Fury (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Fury'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Backlash]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi: Backlash (audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Mercy Kill (audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Aleph-Klasse Sternenjäger |es=Caza estelar clase Aleph |fr=Chasseur de classe Aleph |pl=Myśliwiec typu Aleph }} [[Category:Sienar Fleet Systems products]] [[Category:Starfighter classes]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Alliance]]
